root_text.sn13.6:1.2: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, mahāpathavī parikkhayaṁ pariyādānaṁ gaccheyya, ṭhapetvā satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā.
root_text.sn13.6:1.4: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ, yaṁ vā mahāpathaviyā parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādiṇṇaṁ yā vā satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā avasiṭṭhā”ti?
root_text.sn13.6:2.1: “Etadeva, bhante, bahutaraṁ, mahāpathaviyā, yadidaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādiṇṇaṁ;
root_text.sn13.6:2.3: Neva satimaṁ kalaṁ upenti na sahassimaṁ kalaṁ upenti na satasahassimaṁ kalaṁ upenti mahāpathaviyā parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādiṇṇaṁ upanidhāya satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā avasiṭṭhā”ti.

translation_text.sn13.6:1.2: “Mendicants, suppose the great earth was worn away and eroded except for seven clay balls the size of jujube seeds.
translation_text.sn13.6:1.4: Which is more: the great earth that has been worn away and eroded, or the seven clay balls the size of jujube seeds that are left?”
translation_text.sn13.6:2.1: “Sir, the great earth that has been worn away and eroded is certainly more.
translation_text.sn13.6:2.2: The seven clay balls the size of jujube seeds are tiny.
translation_text.sn13.6:2.3: Compared to the great earth that has been worn away and eroded, it’s not nearly a hundredth, a thousandth, or a hundred thousandth part.”
translation_text.sn13.6:2.4: “In the same way, for a noble disciple, the suffering that’s over and done with is more …”
